title: llame instance configuration
description: Operator-owned instance configuration for llame (config-as-code, openspec/changes/instance-config + providers-and-models-as-code). Optional; a setting absent from this file takes its documented built-in default (there is no bare environment-variable fallback — the environment reaches settings only via {env:...} tokens written in this file). String values may contain {env:NAME}, {env:NAME:-default}, and {path:LOCATION} interpolation tokens, resolved once at boot. This document is both the editor-facing schema (referenced via the top-level $schema key) and the boot-time validator — the same artifact, so they cannot drift.

interpolationToken.description: A whole-value interpolation token: {env:NAME}, {env:NAME:-default}, or {path:LOCATION}. Only legal on non-string settings as the ENTIRE value (no surrounding text) — string settings may embed tokens anywhere instead.

numberOrToken.description: A positive integer, or a whole-value interpolation token that resolves to one.

nullableNumberOrToken.description: A positive integer, null, or a whole-value interpolation token that resolves to one of those.

nullableString.description: A plain string, or null to explicitly unset (overriding any legacy environment fallback). May embed {env:...}/{path:...} tokens anywhere in the string.

heartbeatSecondsOrToken.description: The job-queue's native worker-liveness window: a positive integer >= 10 (pg-boss's own floor for queue heartbeatSeconds), or a whole-value interpolation token that resolves to one.

providerType.description: Executable provider client implementation. Only "openai" is supported in this slice (native OpenAI + any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, e.g. Ollama). Anthropic is a follow-up: this enum is strict-closed so a config naming an unimplemented type fails validation, not execution.

providerEntry.description: A single provider connection. Providers are duplicable — two entries of the same `type` with distinct `id`s (and typically distinct `baseUrl`s) coexist, e.g. hosted OpenAI alongside a local Ollama.

id.description: Operator-chosen identifier, unique within `providers[]`. Referenced by `models[].provider`.

key.description: Provider credential. Absent, null, or a resolved-empty `{env:...}` token means keyless (e.g. a local Ollama with no auth) — the client is constructed without raising a missing-credential error.

baseUrl.description: Provider endpoint base URL. Absent/null uses the client implementation's own default (OpenAI's hosted API for `type: "openai"`).

modelPricing.description: Per-million-token USD pricing. Display/telemetry metadata only — never affects executability.

modelEntry.description: A single executable model catalog entry.

id.description: Opaque llame model id, e.g. "system:openai:gpt-5.4-mini". Clients treat this as opaque; it is never parsed for routing.

provider.description: References a `providers[].id`. An id not defined there fails boot naming this model and the dangling reference.

providerModelId.description: The server-only model id sent to the provider. Never derived from `id`.

contextWindowTokens.description: Required, execution-critical: sizes the context-compaction trigger (x COMPACTION_WINDOW_RATIO) unless `compactionThresholdTokens` overrides it.

compactionThresholdTokens.description: Optional explicit compaction trigger threshold for this model, overriding the `contextWindowTokens x COMPACTION_WINDOW_RATIO` default. Internal execution config — not returned by `GET /api/v1/models`.

$schema.description: Reference to this published schema, for editor autocomplete/hover. Ignored by the loader — the sole exemption from the closed schema below.

defaults.description: Instance-level model pointers. Deliberately not under the `models` key, which holds the executable catalog itself. Each pointer, when set, must reference a `models[].id` or startup fails.

modelId.description: Default model id used for new chats when the user hasn't chosen one. Must reference an entry in `models[]` when set (boot failure otherwise); unset means model selection fails as a server configuration error at request time.

titleGenerationModelId.description: Model id used to generate chat titles. Must reference an entry in `models[]` when set (boot failure otherwise); unset means title generation is skipped.

providers.description: Provider connections (providers-and-models-as-code, #167). Supersedes the OPENAI_API_KEY / OPENAI_BASE_URL bare environment variables — those names remain valid {env:...} interpolation inputs for an entry's key/baseUrl.

models.description: The executable model catalog (providers-and-models-as-code, #167), superseding the formerly hardcoded server catalog. Each entry's `provider` must reference a `providers[].id`.

runs.description: Durable run execution timers and limits (#48/#50; liveness collapsed onto the job-queue's native heartbeat, durable-run-workers D7). No compaction.* or context-window setting exists here — compaction is model-driven, never an instance knob.

maxOutputTokens.description: Reserved: cap on generated output tokens per model call. Not yet enforced — no run currently reads this value; it is consumed by the run-budget feature (#91). File-only (no legacy env var). Setting it today has no runtime effect.

heartbeatSeconds.description: The job-queue's native worker-liveness window, in seconds: while a run's job is in flight the worker auto-signals liveness at half this interval, and the queue's monitor fails+retries the job if the signal lapses this long — worker-death detection, not an application heartbeat. Falls back to RUN_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS, then 15.

timeoutSeconds.description: In-process wall-clock budget, in seconds: while its worker is alive, a run exceeding this is aborted in-process and recorded as a terminal run.expired (distinct from a user run.cancelled). Falls back to RUN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, then 300.

trustProxy.description: Express trust-proxy setting: a non-negative integer hop count, 'true'/'false', or a subnet spec. Falls back to TRUST_PROXY, then unset (no proxy trusted — client IPs are taken at face value).

poolSize.description: Per-process postgres pool `max`. A run holds a connection per runAs transaction, so set this >= the process's total run concurrency (sum of the active worker profile's group concurrencies) plus HTTP headroom; poolSize x replicas must stay within Postgres max_connections. Falls back to DB_POOL_SIZE, then 10.

tools.description: Tool-calling loop availability (openspec/changes/tool-calling-loop). The operator allowlist is the entire availability story this slice — no policy engine exists yet (D3).

allowed.description: Registered tool ids admitted for advertisement/execution. Default: empty array (no tools advertised or executable — fail closed). An id not registered in the code-owned tool registry fails boot naming the path and id.

maxStepsPerRun.description: Hard cap on tool-requesting steps per run before the loop stops offering tools and drives the model to answer from accumulated context. Falls back to the built-in default, 8.

callTimeoutSeconds.description: Global timeout, in seconds, for a single tool call (a tool may declare a per-tool override at registration — a code-level property, not a config key). Falls back to the built-in default, 15.

workers.description: Worker profiles (durable-run-workers D2/D4): profile name -> the fixed consumer groups it consumes and each one's main-queue concurrency, selected at boot by LLAME_WORKER_PROFILE (default 'all'). A profile referencing an unknown group name fails boot. Built-in 'all' (every group at concurrency 1) and 'web' (no groups) are always available; an entry here replaces that profile name wholesale.

additionalProperties.description: One worker profile: consumer-group name -> main-queue concurrency (positive integer).
